page: Amazon Web Services m7i.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 virtual CPUs and 32 physical cores based on the x86_64 Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities across multiple domains. The instance achieves top-tier results in Geekbench image processing and strong performance in Geekbench single-core, multi-core, and software compilation tests. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 5182.9 GB/sec and large block uncached write speeds at 94.16 GB/sec. CPU-bound tasks benefit from advanced vector and matrix extensions, yielding strong results in Passmark encryption, physics, and database operations. Redis SET operations reach 75.63 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is measured at 76.27 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m7i.16xlarge offers a balanced resource density of 4 GB RAM per vCPU, making it a viable option for workloads requiring modern DDR5 memory speeds without the premium of memory-optimized classes. However, the lack of local NVMe storage means all disk I/O must traverse the network, which represents a tradeoff for storage-heavy applications. This instance is suited for enterprise applications, database operations, web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 256.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The instance delivers strong performance across memory bandwidth, database operations, and image processing benchmarks, supported by advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. It achieves 75.63 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 76.27 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. While cost-efficient for balanced compute and memory tasks, the absence of local storage represents a tradeoff for I/O-intensive applications. It is well-suited for enterprise applications, software compilation, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering strong database and web serving performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance combining sixty-four Intel Xeon vCPUs and high-speed DDR5 memory for enterprise workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor
256.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 5 GHz
Strong performance in database operations, web serving, and image processing
Supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instruction sets for accelerated computing
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage included
Suited for enterprise applications, software compilation, and database workloads
